_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Thursday March 5th | 5:27

Bonjour Tremblant,

Spring Break continues with temperatures staying in our favor! Excellent mountain conditions await, and our mountain crews have been hard at work to deliver 102 trails ready to be discovered. Come enjoy the slopes and make the most of Spring Break on the mountain. 

Don’t forget to check out our 4 incredible terrain parks! From beginner-friendly features to more advanced setups, there’s something for every freestyle rider. Grab your crew, lap your favorites, and make the most of it!  

First Tracks
  • Access to designated lifts, on both North and South side, as early as 7:45 a.m.
  • Ski on designated North Side slopes at 8 a.m.
Alpine Touring & Snowshoeing
This morning, the alpine touring and snowshoe trails are firm and occasionally icy. We encourage you to use caution and adjust your equipment and speed accordingly.

Enjoy every run, have fun… and ski safely! 

Mountain safety tip of the week: Slow down in reduced‑speed zones.
Reduced‑speed areas mean it’s time to slow down for your safety and the safety of others.
Remember, you’re sharing the slopes with other skiers and riders. Stay cautious at all times.
Some zones marked in yellow on the trail map require reduced speed. Please be considerate, and ski or ride slowly in these areas out of respect for the higher traffic.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Happy Thursday! Spring Break continues with temperatures staying in our favor! With a few days since our last snowfall, runs are hard and fast — stay in control and ski smart! Alpine Touring & Snowshoeing: The alpine touring and snowshoe trails are currently firm and at times icy. We encourage you to use caution and adjust your equipment and speed accordingly. Enjoy every run, have fun… and ski safely!
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Joyeux jeudi! La relâche se poursuit avec des températures qui continuent de jouer en notre faveur! Les pistes sont fermes et rapides — demeurez en contrôle et skiez prudemment ! Les sentiers de randonnée alpine et de raquette sont actuellement durcis et parfois glacés. Nous vous invitons à faire preuve de prudence et à adapter votre équipement ainsi que votre vitesse en conséquence. Skiez prudemment!
